Member-only story

Spreading your bets thin feels safe. Charlie Munger called it “diworsification” — safety theater, not actual safety.

2 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Image Generated By Ai
• Diversification Has a Sweet Spot
A reasonable amount of diversification reduces risk. Past that point, you’re not reducing risk anymore — you’re diluting your best ideas with your average ones, and average ideas drag down your returns without meaningfully protecting you.
2. Concentration Built Most Fortunes
Look at how most wealth is actually created — a business, a concentrated stock position, a focused skill — and you’ll find concentration, not a spread-everywhere portfolio. Diversification protects wealth. It rarely creates it.
